mnemonics: pads for passwords, secure data too
one-time, secure, and unique, that's what you do
keyPoints: A One-time Pad is a type of encryption where a plaintext message is encrypted using a random keyword or key that is used only once.
It is considered unbreakable if the key is truly random and of the same length as the message.
The One-time Pad can be used for secure communication over insecure channels, such as phone lines or email.
It is not a symmetric encryption algorithm, meaning the same key is used for both encryption and decryption.
The One-time Pad is vulnerable to key reuse and key compromise, making it impractical for real-world use.
